source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane Boom

actions_taken: Crane immediately placed out of service
Onsite investigation conducted by BSEE Lafayette District
Documents, witness statements and photographs gathered
event.summary: On September 26, 2020, at approximately 1300 hours, a crane operator on Cox Operating's EI 361A facility discovered the boom section closest to the cab was bent. A construction crew was offloading equipment using the platform crane. A 6' x 12' basket of scaffolding weighing approximately 16,300 lb was placed directly under the crane, causing the boom to be oriented at a vertical angle only inches away from the boom angle kick out. The crane operator lowered the load too fast, causing the boom to forcibly contact the boom angle kick out, resulting in structural damage to the crane boom. The estimated damage was $51,600.

threats.name: Crane operator lowers load at unsafe speed
Boom positioned too close to boom angle kick out

controls.name: API RP 2D Operating Practices - Operator Awareness of Load Velocity and Weight
controls.barrier_role: Procedural guidance to prevent shock load during load lowering

supporting_text: The crane operator failed to lower the load at a safe speed which would have prevented the boom from contacting the boom angle kick out.
As per API RP 2D 3.2.3 Moving the Load: d. 'The Crane Operator should be aware of the effect of velocity and weight of the load when lowering to minimize shock load.'

controls.name: Crane operator knowledge of crane operating characteristics
controls.barrier_role: Operator competence in understanding mechanical crane behavior

supporting_text: As per API RP 2D 3.1.5 Operating Practices b. The Crane Operator should be aware of the operating characteristics of the crane.

controls.name: Boom cradle positioning and deck space management
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent boom contact with boom angle kick out

supporting_text: Construction equipment including a 6' x 12' basket of scaffolding weighing approximately 16,300 lb was being placed directly under the crane causing the boom to be oriented at a vertical angle only inches away for the boom angle kick out.
The basket of scaffolding was placed under the crane due to the limited space left on the deck from the amount of equipment offloaded.

controls.name: Visual inspection of crane boom after operations
controls.barrier_role: Detection of structural damage to crane boom

supporting_text: When the crane operator returned to operate the crane, he noticed the bends in the boom's heel section.
When the crane operator returned to the crane to conduct operations, he noticed the damage and immediately placed out of service until the boom could be replaced.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
